top_stories.title: Transformers Remember First, Forget Last: Dual-Process Interference in LLMs
top_stories.summary: arXiv:2603.00270v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: When large language models encounter conflicting information in context, which memories survive -- early or recent? We adapt classical interference paradigms from cognitive psychology to answer this question, testing 39 LLMs across diverse architectures and scales. Every model shows the same pattern
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Adds empirical validation to approaches already used in production AI stacks.
top_stories.primary_risk: Risk of overfitting conclusions to narrow benchmarks.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: Practical improvements when layered onto existing AI workflows.

top_stories.title: Phi-4-reasoning-vision and the lessons of training a multimodal reasoning model
top_stories.summary: We are pleased to announce Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B, a 15 billion parameter open‑weight multimodal reasoning model, available through Microsoft Foundry (opens in new tab), HuggingFace (opens in new tab) and GitHub (opens in new tab). Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B is a broadly capable model that can be used for a wide array of vision-language tasks s
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Reinforces known techniques with modest refinements to existing methods.
top_stories.primary_risk: Marginal performance gains may not justify integration effort.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: Foundation for future optimization rather than immediate disruption.

top_stories.title: US military uses Anthropic's Claude for AI-driven strike planning in Iran war
top_stories.summary: In the war against Iran, the US military is using generative AI at scale for target selection and strike planning for the first time. Of all models, it's the one from the company Washington just banned. The article US military uses Anthropic's Claude for AI-driven strike planning in Iran war appeared first on The Decoder.
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Signals incremental evolution rather than a paradigm shift in current AI systems.
top_stories.primary_risk: Risk of overfitting conclusions to narrow benchmarks.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: Practical improvements when layered onto existing AI workflows.

top_stories.title: AI agents prefer Bitcoin shaping new finance architecture
top_stories.summary: AI agents prefer Bitcoin for digital wealth storage, forcing finance chiefs to adapt their architecture for machine autonomy. When AI systems gain economic autonomy, their internal logic dictates how corporate capital flows. Non-partisan research by the Bitcoin Policy Institute evaluated how these frontier models would transact if operating as inde
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Agent orchestration is emerging as a new software abstraction layer.
top_stories.primary_risk: Debugging complexity and cascading failures in agentic systems.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: End-to-end automation of complex cognitive workflows.

top_stories.title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
top_stories.summary: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.
top_stories.technical_takeaway: Adds empirical validation to approaches already used in production AI stacks.
top_stories.primary_risk: Unclear production readiness despite promising early results.
top_stories.primary_opportunity: Selective adoption in niche use cases with clear ROI.
